text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, there was a little old owl named Oliver. Olivers loved nothing more than hunting through the autumn leaves. One day, he decided to seek out an old map he'd heard whispered about deep in the Forbidden Forest. The map promised wonders old tales hadn't mentioned.

Oliver flew south, past familiar woodland paths. The forest smelled different this time. There were lots of colours. Everywhere he looked was beautiful. That's when he saw them: the glimmering stones. They fluttered gently.

With curiosity, Oliver swooped down and plucked one. It was ruby red. He held it close. It sparkled. Then he flew higher. There were more.

They were all like gems. They glittered like stars.

Olivers gathered many. He was so pleased. He flew back home.

His feathers glimmered.

He rested there, feeling content.

That night, those little gems twinkled softly.

And life wasn't so bad.

Just a
